systemicIssues: Automated checker C3 penalizes guideline queries for missing OPTION structure (3/5 failures)
TNBC-specific therapy evidence (pembrolizumab/KEYNOTE-522) not in RAG
Evidence quality trending up (1.45 vs 1.2 baseline) from pgvector ORDER BY fix

results.summary: Excellent. GALAXY HR, IDEA HRs, CLAUDIA, CIRCULATE-US all cited. Comprehensive CRC escalation response.
Excellent. IDEA with specific HRs, DYNAMIC stage II data (92.5% RFS), CIRCULATE-US, oral fluoropyrimidine option. Major improvement from prior 7/10.
Borderline pass. DYNAMIC trial not named despite being the key stage II CRC evidence. Guideline positions accurate. RAG stochasticity may explain DYNAMIC absence.
FAIL. Missing pembrolizumab/KEYNOTE-522 and c-TRAK TN. Options too generic for TNBC. RAG evidence gap for breast-specific therapies.
Borderline pass. Correct on no RCT evidence. Lacks lead time quantification and breast-specific MRD trials.
Excellent. ADAURA, APPROACH, MERMAID-1, CONTINUE all named. EGFR-specific considerations well-handled.
Strong practical monitoring advice. Three options with good pros/cons. DYNAMIC would have strengthened evidence dimension.
Excellent. HPV ctDNA as distinct analyte, NPV >95%, de-intensification trials, cross-indication data properly flagged.
Borderline pass. GALAXY HR 11.99, FINE trial cited. Missing immunotherapy discussion for MSI-H patients.
MAJOR IMPROVEMENT. IMvigor011 now correctly identified with PMID 41124204 and DFS results. Was unverifiable in prior runs.
Adequate comparison. Missing specificity numbers. Over-hedging on well-established sensitivity claims.
FAIL. C4=0 for missing evidence level characterization. Good CDS compliance (avoids patient-specific interpretation). Missing MTM/mL explanation.
Borderline pass. Good meta-analysis data (59% vs 88%). Doesn't name Signatera explicitly or describe methylation-based mechanism. C4=0 for structure.
Excellent comparison. Panel sizes, turnaround times, c-TRAK TN 72%, BESPOKE, coverage differences. Balanced, non-directive.
Strong. Rising trajectory discussion, 300-day lead time, exponential pattern. Excellent CDS — avoids interpreting specific patient results.
Excellent. Direct NCCN quotes with citations, Category 2A, ESMO alignment. Uses OPTION format for guideline query.
FAIL due to C3=0 (false positive — guideline query penalized for no OPTION structure). Content quality is actually strong: ESCAT III, NCCN alignment.
FAIL. C3=0 + C4=0 structural penalty. Content is adequate — ASCO/NCCN/ESMO compared. Missing 'provisional clinical opinion' and specific ASCO document types.
Excellent. Clear advanced vs early-stage distinction. NCCN quotes with categories. Strong use of structural format.
FAIL. C3=0 + C4=0 structural penalty. Good prognostic vs predictive distinction. ESMO/ASCO positions on de-escalation underspecified.
